data.title: Does WEX's (WEX) Embedded B2B Payments Push Mark a Shift in Its Core Investment Story?
data.description:
• Recently, WEX highlighted its expectations that embedded payments will gain momentum in 2026, especially in business-to-business transactions, as this technology becomes a standard feature in corporate payment flows.
  • At the same time, WEX’s favorable valuation metrics, improving earnings outlook versus peers, and alignment with broader embedded payments adoption have drawn investor attention as markets shift toward value-oriented financial stocks.
  • We’ll now examine how WEX’s focus on embedded B2B payments adoption could influence the company’s existing investment narrative and future prospects.

WEX Investment Narrative Recap

To own WEX, you need to believe in its ability to shift from traditional fuel cards toward integrated, digital corporate payments without eroding profitability. The latest embedded payments commentary supports the existing catalyst around B2B payment adoption, but it does not materially change the near term risk that competition from fintechs and large platforms could pressure transaction margins and slow earnings progress.

Among recent announcements, WEX’s integration with the Tesla Supercharger network stands out, because it directly addresses the risk of declining legacy fuel volumes by expanding into EV-focused mobility payments. This move fits with the same embedded and digital payments theme that underpins WEX’s growth catalysts in Corporate Payments and Mobility, reinforcing the idea that its long term story now depends far less on traditional fuel spend and more on how effectively it scales diversified payment flows.

Yet, while the opportunity around embedded B2B and EV payments is appealing, investors should be aware that intensified competition across payments platforms could still...

data.title: Assessing Ford Motor (F) Valuation As EV Reset Charge And Hybrid Pivot Reshape Expectations
data.description:
Ford Motor (F) is in the spotlight after announcing a US$19.5b special charge linked to its electric vehicle reset, as the automaker leans into hybrids and extended range models, and explores battery storage opportunities.

See our latest analysis for Ford Motor.

Ford's reset on electric vehicles, analyst upgrades and a recent 52 week high have come alongside a 90 day share price return of 21.89% and a 1 year total shareholder return of 57.08%, suggesting momentum has strengthened over both shorter and longer horizons.

If Ford's pivot has you rethinking the auto space, it could be a good moment to scan other auto manufacturers that match your view on electrification and hybrids.

After a 57.08% 1 year total return and a recent analyst upgrade, Ford now trades slightly above the average analyst price target and at an intrinsic premium. This raises the question: is there still a buying opportunity here, or is future growth already priced in?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 13.5% Overvalued

Ford Motor's most followed narrative puts fair value at US$12.52 per share, compared with the last close of US$14.20, framing current pricing as rich.

The analysts have a consensus price target of $10.8 for Ford Motor based on their expectations of its future earnings growth, profit margins and other risk factors. However, there is a degree of disagreement amongst analysts, with the most bullish reporting a price target of $16.0, and the most bearish reporting a price target of just $8.0.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what justifies paying up for a company with flat revenue assumptions yet materially higher margins and earnings five years out? The key is how profit mix, recurring software style revenue and a lower future earnings multiple all interact. If you want to see exactly how those pieces are stitched together, the full narrative lays out the numbers in detail.

Result: Fair Value of $12.52 (OVERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, persistent EV division losses, along with pressure from tariffs and input costs, could still unsettle margins and challenge the case for paying a premium today.

Find out about the key risks to this Ford Motor narrative.

Another View: Earnings Multiple Sends a Different Signal

If you step away from fair value models and just look at earnings, Ford trades on a P/E of 12x versus 18.6x for the global auto group, 24.1x for peers and a fair ratio of 28.7x. That gap points to valuation risk running the other way. The question is which story you trust more.

data.title: Is Glencore (LSE:GLEN) Fairly Priced After Recent Share Price Strength?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Glencore is attractively priced or starting to look expensive, its recent share moves and valuation checks offer some useful clues.
  • The stock last closed at £4.53, with returns of 8.0% over 7 days, 20.5% over 30 days, 10.7% year to date and 27.9% over 1 year. The 3 year return stands at a 5.2% decline and the 5 year return at 109.2%.
  • Recent news coverage has focused on Glencore's position in global commodities markets and how investor sentiment around resources companies has shifted over time. Along with headlines about capital allocation and portfolio positioning, this context helps explain why the share price has moved the way it has.
  • On Simply Wall St's valuation checks, Glencore scores 3 out of 6. We will unpack this using multiple valuation approaches, then finish with a framework that can help you think about value in an even more rounded way.

Find out why Glencore's 27.9% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Glencore Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting them back to today, so you can compare that value with the current share price.

For Glencore, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is around US$986.6m. Analysts provide free cash flow estimates for the earlier years, and Simply Wall St extends these projections further out, with year by year assumptions through to 2035. Within this, free cash flow in 2030 is projected at US$4.566b, with discounted values provided for each year in between.

Bringing all those discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of £4.20 per share, compared with the recent share price of £4.53. On this basis, the DCF suggests Glencore is about 7.8% overvalued, which is a relatively small premium to the modelled value.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

Glencore is fairly valued according to our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), but this can change at a moment's notice. Track the value in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted on when to act.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Glencore.

Approach 2: Glencore Price vs Sales

For Glencore, the preferred yardstick is the Price to Sales, or P/S, ratio, which is often useful for companies where revenue is a key reference point and earnings can be volatile. It lets you see how much investors are paying for each unit of sales, regardless of short term profit swings.

What counts as a normal P/S ratio depends on what the market expects for growth and how much risk it sees in those future sales. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk usually support a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher risk tend to justify a lower one.

Glencore currently trades on a P/S of 0.31x, compared with the Metals and Mining industry average of 3.37x and a peer average of 4.78x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Glencore is 1.10x. This Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of what the P/S might be, given factors such as earnings growth, industry, profit margin, market cap and risks. It is more tailored than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for those company specific characteristics.

Compared with that Fair Ratio of 1.10x, Glencore’s current 0.31x suggests the shares may be undervalued on this measure.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/S ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1445 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Glencore Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. On Simply Wall St you can use Narratives to link your view of Glencore’s story with a financial forecast and a fair value, by setting out your assumptions for future revenue, earnings and margins. You can then compare your Fair Value to the current share price on the Community page, where Narratives are updated when new news or earnings arrive. Different investors can, for example, plug in a higher fair value closer to £4.61 if they think Glencore’s growth and profitability will track the more optimistic assumptions, or a lower fair value nearer £3.09 if they focus more on the risks and prefer the cautious case. This provides a clear, numbers backed way to decide whether the stock looks attractive or stretched against your own view.

Do you think there's more to the story for Glencore? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Assessing AMD (NasdaqGS:AMD) Valuation After CES 2026 AI Announcements And Recent Share Price Volatility
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is back in the spotlight after a cluster of AI focused announcements at CES 2026, including new MI440X and MI455X data center chips and fresh partnerships across automotive and edge computing.

See our latest analysis for Advanced Micro Devices.

Despite the CES headlines, AMD’s share price has been under pressure recently, with a 7 day share price return of 8.10% and a 90 day share price return of 6.84%. At the same time, the 1 year total shareholder return sits at 73.18%, suggesting long term momentum remains strong while short term sentiment has cooled.

If AMD’s AI push has your attention, it could be a good moment to broaden your radar with high growth tech and AI stocks that are also riding the artificial intelligence trend.

With AMD shares recently pulling back even as AI headlines keep coming, investors are asking a simple question: is this AI leader still undervalued, or is the market already pricing in years of future growth?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 24.8% Undervalued

According to oscargarcia, the narrative implies a fair value of about US$270 per share versus AMD’s last close at US$203.17, framing the current price as materially below that estimate.

AMD has evolved into a formidable player in AI and enterprise compute, propelled by leadership in CPUs (EPYC) and a growing presence in GPUs (Instinct MI series). With solid revenue and earnings growth, strong analyst upgrades, and a valuation that still looks reasonable compared to peers, AMD offers a balanced play on AI infrastructure growth.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what turns that AI and data center story into a higher fair value than today’s price? data.title: How Investors May Respond To Amphenol (APH) Adding Linde CEO Sanjiv Lamba To Its Board
data.description:

  • Lamba’s long tenure running complex multinational operations across the Americas, APAC, and EMEA introduces deep international and manufacturing expertise that could influence how Amphenol oversees its global expansion and governance priorities.

data.title: The Bull Case For Apple (AAPL) Could Change Following China Risk Pushback And Finance Leadership Shift
data.description:
• In early January 2026, Apple Inc. filed its definitive proxy statement urging shareholders to vote against a proposal for a formal report on risks and costs tied to its exposure to the People’s Republic of China, while also appointing long-time insider Ben Borders as its new Principal Accounting Officer.
  • This combination of resistance to a China-focused risk audit and leadership reshuffling in finance comes as investors increasingly question Apple’s AI direction, competitive position, and supply-chain concentration.
  • We’ll now examine how Apple’s pushback against a China risk review, amid AI concerns, could affect the company’s broader investment narrative.

Apple Investment Narrative Recap

To own Apple today, you need to believe its ecosystem, services, and AI upgrades can offset concerns about competition, regulation, and hardware maturity. The short term catalyst still sits with how convincingly Apple refreshes its AI story, while the biggest risk remains regulatory and political exposure around China and the App Store. The latest proxy fight over a China risk report and the appointment of a new Principal Accounting Officer do not materially change those near term drivers.

One announcement that does matter here is Apple’s decision to oppose the shareholder proposal for a formal China risk review ahead of the February 24 AGM. That choice sits uncomfortably beside investor worries about supply chain concentration and tariffs, even as the market focuses on Apple Intelligence and a potential Siri overhaul as the next upgrade catalyst.

data.title: Does BILL (BILL) Reentry by Parnassus Hint at a Deeper Shift in Its Margin Story?
data.description:
• In recent days, Parnassus Core Equity Fund reinitiated a position in BILL Holdings, highlighting the company’s role as a leading financial operations platform for small and midsize businesses and emphasizing its focus on improving operating margins.
  • The fund’s endorsement, based on BILL’s partnerships, network effects with accounting firms, and long growth runway from workflow digitization, underscores how institutional confidence can influence broader investor perception.
  • We’ll now examine how Parnassus’s renewed backing, centered on BILL’s operating margin focus, could reshape the company’s existing investment narrative.

BILL Holdings Investment Narrative Recap

To own BILL Holdings, you need to believe small and midsize businesses will keep adopting digital financial workflows and that BILL can translate that usage into improving profitability. Parnassus Core Equity Fund’s renewed position reinforces confidence in BILL’s operating margin focus, but it does not materially change the key near term catalyst around AI driven product adoption or the biggest current risk of competitive pressure from large fintech and software platforms.

The most relevant recent development here is Parnassus reinitiating its position, explicitly pointing to BILL’s role as a financial operations platform for SMBs, its deep partner network and management’s stated attention to operating margins. That endorsement sits alongside earlier signals such as activist pressure for cost discipline and potential strategic alternatives, adding another institutional voice focusing investors on execution around margins rather than changing the underlying growth drivers.
